label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which statement is true according to the passage. First, I'll go through each option and check the passage. Option A: "The lottery system is certainly a good way to allocate school places." The passage mentions that the government is reviewing the use of lotteries because many children aren't getting their first-choice schools. It also says that in Brighton and Hove, there was a 3.5% increase in first-choice placements after introducing the lottery, but over 5% got a school not on their list. However, the passage doesn't state that the lottery is "certainly a good way." The government is reviewing it, which suggests they're not sure yet. So A might not be correct. Option B: "Over one in five children fail to get into their first-choice schools in parts of Britain." The first sentence of the passage says "more than 20 percent of children are failing to get into their first-choice schools in parts of the country." Since 20% is one in five, this matches. So B seems correct. Option C: "The lottery system is welcomed by parents around twenty-five local authorities." The passage says lotteries are used in around twenty-five local authorities, but it doesn't mention whether parents welcome it.
